title: Input and Query Nodes (InputPosition, InputNormal, InputIndex, InputRadius)
description: Implement 4 context-reading nodes that provide per-element data from the current mesh being processed. InputPosition outputs the position of the current vertex. InputNormal outputs the normal at the current vertex/face. InputIndex outputs the index of the current element. InputRadius outputs the distance from the origin. These are used in field expressions -- e.g., 'move vertices outward by their distance from center' would wire InputPosition -> VectorMath(length) -> ScaleElements.
definition-of-done: 1. Four node classes in nodes/input/. 2. Each reads from the evaluation context's current mesh and element index. 3. InputPosition outputs VECTOR3 vertex position. 4. InputNormal outputs VECTOR3 normal (per-vertex or per-face depending on context domain). 5. InputIndex outputs INT element index. 6. InputRadius outputs FLOAT distance from origin. 7. All work correctly when evaluated per-element in a field context.
testing-plan: 1. InputPosition on a unit cube: evaluate at vertex 0, verify position matches. 2. InputNormal on a cube face: verify normal is axis-aligned. 3. InputIndex: evaluate across all vertices, verify 0..N-1 sequence. 4. InputRadius on a unit sphere: verify all outputs are ~1.0.
todos: Define how NodeContext exposes current element index and mesh for per-element evaluation
unknowns: Whether per-element evaluation should be explicit (loop in the node) or implicit (framework evaluates the node once per element)
